Trust & Security Centre

Built to pass your IT/OT review.
Built to keep passing it.

ISO 27001 certified. Cellular architecture. No corporate network dependency. Every claim on this page is audited, documented, and available for download. We built SAM4's security posture for one audience: the person who has to sign off.

Certifications

Audited. Certified. Documented.

These aren't logo badges. Each certification has a defined scope, an independent auditor, and documentation you can hand directly to your security team.

ISO 27001

Information security management system certification. Covers risk assessment, access control policies, incident management, and continuous improvement of security practices across the organisation.

Scope: Organisation-wide information security management

ISO 9001

Quality management system certification. Ensures consistent processes for product development, service delivery, and customer support. Relevant for enterprise procurement that requires QMS compliance.

Scope: Product development, service delivery, customer operations

NIS2

NIS2 Aligned

Aligned with the EU Network and Information Security Directive (NIS2). Relevant for customers in critical infrastructure sectors (water, energy, chemicals) where NIS2 compliance is mandatory for suppliers. Gap closure programme in progress for 2026.

Scope: Critical infrastructure supply chain requirements

Network architecture

Cellular, not corporate. The single most important architectural decision.

SAM4 data travels over cellular networks (4G/LTE) from the gateway to the cloud via HTTPS or MQTT, encrypted with TLS 1.2. Your corporate network (WiFi, LAN, SCADA, OT) is never touched. This is the point that changes the security conversation.

No firewall changes

The gateway communicates outbound over cellular. No inbound connections to your network. No firewall rules to create, no ports to open, no VPN tunnels to configure.

No corporate WiFi or LAN

The data path is entirely separate from your IT and OT networks. There is no logical or physical connection between the SAM4 gateway and your corporate infrastructure.

No IT involvement for deployment

Because the network is cellular, your IT team doesn't need to provision anything. No network architecture review (for connectivity), no change requests, no IT project timeline to manage.

OT network isolation preserved

SAM4 never connects to the OT network. The CT/VT clamps are passive sensors: they read current and voltage. They do not inject signals, send commands, or interact with motor controllers.

The cellular architecture isn't just a convenience. It's the reason SAM4 can be deployed in weeks instead of the months that IT/OT convergence projects typically require.

Data security

Encrypted in transit. Encrypted at rest. Audited continuously.

The specifics your security review will ask for: addressed directly.

Encryption in transit

All data transmitted from the gateway to the cloud uses TLS 1.2 encryption over the cellular connection via HTTPS or MQTT.

Encryption at rest

All stored data is encrypted using AES-256. This covers raw signal data, processed analytics, diagnostic reports, and customer configuration data.

Data residency

SAM4 cloud infrastructure is hosted in the EU (primary) with configurable data residency options for customers with specific geographic requirements. Data sovereignty requirements are addressed during onboarding.

Access controls & audit logging

Role-based access control (RBAC) with SSO integration. All access events are logged and auditable. Administrative actions are tracked with full audit trails. Session management includes automatic timeout and re-authentication.

Your data, your rules

Transparency commitments you can hold us to

These aren't aspirational statements. They're contractual commitments documented in our MSA and verified through independent audit.

100%

You own your data

All raw signal data, processed analytics, and diagnostic outputs belong to you. Full data export available at any time. If you leave, your data leaves with you. Ours is deleted within 90 days.

99.5%

Platform uptime SLA

Contractual platform availability target. Measured monthly. Downtime during scheduled maintenance windows is excluded. Real uptime figures are shared with customers quarterly.

<24h

Security incident response

Contractual notification within 24 hours of a confirmed security incident. Full incident report within 72 hours. Aligned with NIS2 reporting obligations for critical infrastructure suppliers.

EU

Data residency default

All customer data stored and processed within the EU by default. Alternative data residency configurations available for customers with specific geographic or sovereignty requirements.

0

Data resale

Your operational data is never sold, shared with third parties, or used for anything beyond delivering your monitoring service. This includes anonymised or aggregated forms. Zero exceptions.

1/yr

Independent audit cycle

ISO 27001 surveillance audit every 12 months with full re-certification every 3 years. ISO 9001 on the same cycle. Results shared on request.

Security documentation

Download the docs your review requires

Request the specific documents you need. They download immediately. No waiting for a sales follow-up.

Available documents

  • ISO 27001 Certificate
  • Architecture Diagram
  • NIS2 Compliance Summary

Request documents

Which documents do you need? *

Regulatory alignment

Built for regulated industries

SAM4 operates in water utilities, chemical plants, oil & gas facilities, and critical infrastructure. The platform's security posture reflects the regulatory requirements of these sectors.

NIS2 Directive

The EU Network and Information Security Directive requires entities in essential sectors to manage cybersecurity risk across their supply chain. SAM4's certifications and cellular architecture support customer NIS2 compliance obligations. Samotics is progressing its own NIS2 alignment programme through 2026.

OFWAT / Water industry

UK water utilities face regulatory requirements around operational resilience, pollution prevention, and data protection. SAM4's monitoring data supports regulatory reporting while meeting Ofwat's expectations for supplier security.

ATEX / Hazardous environments

SAM4 hardware installs in the safe zone (MCC), not in ATEX-classified areas. No ATEX certification is required for the monitoring hardware because it never enters the hazardous zone.

GDPR / Data protection

SAM4 processes industrial equipment data, not personal data. Where user account data is involved, Samotics complies with GDPR requirements including data minimisation, right to access, and right to deletion.

Pre-answered for your review

The 10 questions your IT/OT team will ask

We've been through enough security reviews to know the pattern. Here are the answers, up front.

✓

Does it connect to our corporate network?

No. SAM4 uses cellular (4G/LTE) connectivity. Your corporate WiFi, LAN, and OT networks are never involved.

✓

Do we need to open firewall ports?

No. The gateway communicates outbound over cellular. No inbound connections, no firewall changes, no VPN tunnels.

✓

Does it touch our SCADA or control systems?

No. The CT/VT clamps are passive sensors. They read current and voltage. They do not inject signals, send commands, or interact with controllers.

✓

What certifications do you hold?

ISO 27001, ISO 9001. Aligned with NIS2. All certificates available for download on this page.

✓

Where is our data stored?

EU by default. AES-256 encrypted at rest. TLS 1.2+ in transit. Data residency options available for specific geographic requirements.

✓

Who has access to our data?

Role-based access control with SSO integration. All access logged and auditable. Only your assigned diagnostics team and authorised Samotics personnel.

✓

Do you share or sell our data?

No. Your data is never shared with third parties, sold, or used for anything beyond delivering your service. This is contractual.

✓

What happens if we terminate?

Full data export provided. All customer data deleted from Samotics systems within 90 days. Documented in the MSA.

✓

How long does IT approval typically take?

Most IT/OT reviews complete in 1–3 weeks. The cellular architecture removes the most common objection: network integration.

✓

Can we do a pen test or security assessment?

Yes. We support customer-initiated security assessments and can share results from recent third-party penetration tests under NDA.

Need to send this checklist to your IT team? Download the full security documentation pack — it includes the architecture diagram, ISO 27001 certificate, and a pre-written IT/OT review brief designed to accelerate internal approvals.

Get the full security documentation: architecture diagrams, data flow, compliance certifications.
